rationale: The citing paper explicitly says it extends prior CRM implementation research and centers on acquisition/maintenance incentives, while the cited paper’s main contribution is defining and measuring CRM initiation/maintenance/termination and linking these processes to performance. That strong construct-level overlap (especially initiation/acquisition and maintenance) makes the citation plausibly foundational/theory-sourcing rather than a generic mention. With only DOI-level match evidence and no context snippet, it is still safer to retain for full relation judgment.
